info.name: Bank Categorizer
info.description:
Manual test collection for the Bank Categorizer API: import bank statements, manage categories, auto/manually categorize transactions, and answer spending questions.

Suggested workflow:
1. Run "Categories > Create Category" a few times (or rely on the default seeded categories created automatically on first app startup).
2. Run "Transactions > Import Transactions" with a sample file from the repo's `samples/` folder.
3. Browse "Transactions > List All Transactions" / "List Uncategorized Transactions", then use "Update Transaction Category" to fix any of them.
4. Use the "Spending" folder to query totals and period comparisons.

All requests use the `{{baseUrl}}` collection variable (defaults to `http://localhost:8080`) plus a few convenience variables (`categoryId`, `transactionId`, dates) you can edit per request or at the collection level.

variable.description: Base URL of the running app
A category id to use in requests below; auto-filled by 'Create Category' if run first
A transaction id to use in PATCH requests; find one via 'List All Transactions'
Start of a spending date range (ISO yyyy-MM-dd)
End of a spending date range (ISO yyyy-MM-dd)
Number of previous months to compare against in the spending/compare endpoint

request.description:
Creates a spending category with optional keywords used for auto-categorization on import. On success, saves the new id into the `categoryId` collection variable for use by the requests below.

Fails with 409 if the name already exists.
Lists all spending categories, including their keywords. Includes the default seeded categories (Groceries, Dining & Takeaway, Transport & Fuel, etc.) if none have been deleted.
Deletes the category identified by `categoryId`. Transactions referencing it become uncategorized rather than being deleted. Returns 204 with no body; 404 if the id doesn't exist.

formdata.description: Select a CSV or XLSX bank statement, e.g. samples/transactions_basic.csv from the repo

request.description:
Uploads a CSV or XLSX bank statement. Rows are parsed into transactions and auto-categorized by matching each description against existing category keywords; malformed rows are skipped rather than failing the import. Try it with samples/transactions_basic.csv or samples/transactions_aliases.xlsx from the repo root. Files over 15MB are rejected with 413.

Remember to click the 'file' field's value and select a file before sending — Postman can't pre-fill a local file path from a shared collection.

Manually adds a single transaction (date, description, signed amount — expenses negative, income positive) and runs it through the same auto-categorization as an imported row. 400 if date, description, or amount is missing/invalid.

query.description: Sort field and direction, e.g. date,asc or amount,desc

request.description: Lists every transaction (categorized and uncategorized), paginated. All three query params are optional (defaults: page=0, size=20, sort=date,desc) — response is a page envelope: `{ content, page, size, totalElements, totalPages }`.

Lists only the transactions with no category assigned — the ones worth reviewing and fixing via 'Update Transaction Category' below. Paginated the same way as 'List All Transactions'. Any `category` value other than 'uncategorized' returns 400.

Manually assigns or corrects the category of the transaction identified by `transactionId` to `categoryId`. Doesn't affect the category's keywords, so it won't change future auto-categorization. 404 if either id doesn't exist.
Returns the total spent in category `categoryId` between `from` and `to` (inclusive, ISO dates). 404 if the category doesn't exist; 400 if the date range is missing or invalid.

Compares category `categoryId`'s spending in the current calendar month against each of the previous `lookback` months, plus their average. Only `period=month` is currently supported; anything else returns 400. `lookback` must be a positive integer no greater than 24; out-of-range values also return 400.

`category` is now optional. Omit it to compare total spending across *all* categories combined instead of a single one; `categoryId`/`categoryName` are then `null` in the response, e.g.:
```json
{
  "categoryId": null,
  "categoryName": null,
  "period": "month",
  "lookback": 3,
  "current": { "label": "2026-07", "from": "2026-07-01", "to": "2026-07-31", "totalSpent": 823.10 },
  "previousPeriods": [ { "label": "2026-06", "from": "2026-06-01", "to": "2026-06-30", "totalSpent": 650.00 } ],
  "previousAverage": 650.00
}
```
To compare a single category instead (existing behavior, unchanged), pass `category` as before.
